name: Philipp Schmid
bio: Philipp Schmid is a Staff Engineer for Developer Experience and Developer Relations at Google DeepMind, building the first AI DevRel/DevX team to bring Google DeepMind's research to every developer. Previously he was Technical Lead at Hugging Face for four years (2021-2025), where he led strategic partnerships with AWS, Google Cloud, Azure, Nvidia, Dell, Cloudflare, and DigitalOcean, helping grow revenue from $0 to ~$100 million through cloud and hardware offerings. He created Hugging Face Inference Endpoints and the SageMaker Hugging Face Inference Toolkit, and collaborated on projects including Zephyr, SmolLM, and StarCoder. He also created IGEL, the first German instruction-tuned Large Language Model. Recognized as the first German AWS Machine Learning Hero in 2021, Schmid is a prolific technical writer with hundreds of blog posts, tutorials, and open-source projects covering LLM fine-tuning, deployment, AI agents, and the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

Spoke at 'Agents in Production 2025' on building agentic AI systems with Google Gemini

Created IGEL — the first German instruction-tuned Large Language Model
Created Hugging Face Inference Endpoints — secure production solution for deploying ML models on dedicated infrastructure

key_contributions.title: Hugging Face Inference Endpoints
key_contributions.description: Created and led as Technical Lead the secure production solution for deploying ML models on dedicated, fully managed infrastructure at Hugging Face

key_contributions.title: gemini-samples
key_contributions.description: Personal samples, snippets, and guides showcasing experiments and implementations using Google DeepMind Gemini models. 1,357 stars on GitHub.

key_contributions.title: mcp-cli
key_contributions.description: Lightweight CLI to interact with MCP servers, making it easy to test and debug Model Context Protocol integrations. 1,028 stars on GitHub.

key_contributions.title: Deep Learning with PyTorch and Hugging Face
key_contributions.description: Comprehensive tutorial collection for getting started with Deep Learning using PyTorch and Hugging Face libraries. 1,360 stars.

key_contributions.title: SageMaker Hugging Face Inference Toolkit
key_contributions.description: Open-source library for serving Hugging Face Transformers models on Amazon SageMaker, enabling enterprise LLM deployment

key_contributions.title: Clipper.js
key_contributions.description: HTML to Markdown converter and crawler for web content extraction. 616 stars on GitHub.

key_contributions.title: IGEL
key_contributions.description: The first German instruction-tuned Large Language Model, capable of sentiment analysis, translation, and other NLP tasks in German

key_contributions.title: AI Agent Benchmark Compendium
key_contributions.description: Compendium of over 50 benchmarks for evaluating AI agents across function calling, tool use, reasoning, coding, and computer interaction. 112 stars.

key_contributions.title: EasyLLM
key_contributions.description: Helpful tools and utilities for working with large language models, simplifying common LLM workflows. 474 stars.

quotes.text: Every good story has to end, and after 4 incredible years at Hugging Face, it's time for me to start my next adventure. When I joined Hugging Face, we were a small team of 20 and the Hub had less than 5,000 models.

It has never been easier to learn how Git works and the Git commands with having LLMs explain you the concepts. It might sound scary now to become a software developer, but I think there never has been a better or easier time to get started.
quotes.source: Dev Interrupted podcast — Google DeepMind's AI playbook for engineering

quotes.text: MCP is an open standard that enables developers to build secure agents and complex workflows on top of LLMs. It tries to decouple components and define clear interfaces.
quotes.source: philschmid.de — MCP with open LLMs, OpenAI or Gemini
